questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The elastic behaviour of material for linear stress and linear strain, is shown in the figure. The energy density for a linear strain of 5×10-4 is _____  kJ m-3. Assume that material is elastic upto the linear strain of 5×10-4,
questions.solution:
From the above graph, we can find that when the strain is 5×10-4 the stress is 100 MPa. We know that energy density is

U=12×stress×strain⇒U=12×100×106×5×10-4

⇒U=2.5×104 J m-3=25 kJ m-3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: When a long glass capillary tube of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>015</mn><mi>cm</mi></math> is dipped in a liquid, the liquid rises to a height of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> within it. If the contact angle between the liquid and glass to close to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>°</mo></math>, the surface tension of the liquid, in milliNewton <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><msub><mi>ρ</mi><mtext>(liqued) </mtext></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>900</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></math> (Given answer in closed integer)
questions.solution:
Using the concept of rise due to surface tension,

 2Tr=hρg

T=rhρg2=15×10-5×15×10-2×900×102=101 milliNewton m-1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A hydraulic press can lift <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> when a mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> is placed on the smaller piston. It can lift kg when the diameter of the larger piston is increased by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math> times and that of the smaller piston is decreased by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math> times keeping the same mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> on the smaller piston.
questions.solution:
Using Pascals law, 

100×gA2=mgA1         ...1

Let m mass can lift M0 in second case then

M0g16A2=mgA1/16          ...2

Since A=πd24

From equation 1 and 2 we get

M016.100=16

⇒M0=25600 kg

questions.q: A square aluminium (shear modulus is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>9</mn></msup><msup><mi>Nm</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>) slab of side <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> and thickness <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> is subjected to a shearing force (on its narrow face) of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>18</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>4</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math>. The lower edge is riveted to the floor. The displacement of the upper edge is _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In an experiment to determine the Young's modulus, steel wires of five different lengths (1,2,3,4 and 5) but of same cross-section 2 mm2 were taken and curves between extension and load were obtained. The slope (extension/load) of the curves were plotted with the wire length and the following graph is obtained. If the Young's modulus of given steel wires is x×1011N m-2, then the value of x is _____ .
questions.solution:
We know that, Young's modulus is Y=FLA∆L, where, F is force or load acting on wire, A is cross-section area, L is length and ∆L is extension in length.

Now, from given graph, the slope of the line is  =ΔLWL=ΔLLW=1YA

⇒Y=1slopeA

Thus, Young's modulus of the steel wires is  Y=12×10-60.25×10-5

⇒Y=2×1011 N m-2.

Therefore, x=2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Two wires each of radius 0.2 cm and negligible mass, one made of steel and the other made of brass are loaded as shown in the figure. The elongation of the steel wire is ______10–6 m. [Young's modulus for steel =2×1011 N m–2 and g=10 m s–2]
questions.solution:
The force acting on steel wire F is 2+1.14 g=3.14g

From the relation of Young's modulus, it can be written

FA=YΔLL

⇒ΔL=FLAY=3.14×g×1.6π×0.22×2×1011×10-4

⇒∆L=2×10-5

⇒∆L=20×10-6 m

Ratio of amplitude of y1 to y2=x:1. The value of x is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The potential energy of a particle of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> in motion along the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>-axis is given by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">U</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>cos</mi><mn>4</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></math>. The time period of the particle for small oscillation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>≃</mo><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></mfrac></mfenced><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>K</mi></math> is _____ .
questions.solution:
Given here, potential energy, U=41-cos4x J

Using the relation between conservative force and potential energy, F=-dUdx.

We have, F=-4+sin4x4=-16sin4x

For small θ, sinθ≈θ.

Acceleration of particle is a=-64xm=-64x4=-16x

As the oscillations are simple harmonic in nature, so  a=-ω2x⇒ω2=16⇒ω=4 rad s-1

Now, time period of oscillation isT=2πω=π2.

Thus, the value of K=2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In the figure given below. a block of mass M=490 g placed on a frictionless table is connected with two springs having same spring constant (K=2 N m-1). If the block is horizontally displaced through X m then the number of complete oscillations it will make in 14π seconds will be ______.
questions.solution:
Both the springs are connected in parallel, therefore

Keq=K+K=2K

Now, time period of spring-block system is given by,

T=2πmKeq=2πm2K

Given here, m=490 gm=0.49 kg and K=2 N m-1

So, T=2π0.492×2

=2π49400=2π720=7π10

Now, number of oscillation in 14π is

N=timeT=14π7π10=20

questions.q: The amplitude of wave disturbance propagating in the positive <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>-direction is given by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi><msup><mo>)</mo><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfrac></math> at time <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mo>)</mo><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfrac></math> at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>s</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi></math> are in metres. The shape of wave does not change during the propagation. The velocity of the wave will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>.</mo></math>

questions.q: Two travelling waves produces a standing wave represented by equation. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi>cos</mi><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>57</mn><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo> </mo><mi>sin</mi><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mfenced><mrow><mn>78</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>. The node closest to the origin in the region <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math> will be at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo> </mo><mfenced><mrow><mtext>in</mtext><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>.</mo></math>

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A wire having a linear mass density <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> is stretched between two rigid supports with a tension of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>900</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo>.</mo></math> The wire resonates at a frequency of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>500</mn><mo> </mo><mi>Hz</mi><mo>.</mo></math> The next higher frequency at which the same wire resonates is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>550</mn><mo> </mo><mi>Hz</mi><mo>.</mo></math> The length of the wire is ___________ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo>.</mo></math>

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Two travelling waves of equal amplitudes and equal frequencies move in opposite directions along a string. They interfere to produce a stationary wave whose equation is given by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>10</mn><mi>cos</mi><mi>π</mi><mi>x</mi><mi>sin</mi><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi></mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mi>cm</mi></math>. The amplitude of the particle at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>4</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> will be _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cm</mi><mo>.</mo></math>
questions.solution:
Amplitude is the maximum displacement from the mean position, for that

sin2πtT=1

Therefore, A=10cos43π=10×12= 5 cm

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The first overtone frequency of an open organ pipe is equal to the fundamental frequency of a closed organ pipe. If the length of the closed organ pipe is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>. The length of the open organ pipe is _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cm</mi></math>
questions.solution:
The fundamental frequency of the closed organ pipe is given by, f0=v4lc.

The frequency of the first overtone of a open organ pipe is given by, f1=2×v2lo.

Since,

f0=f1⇒v4lc=vlo⇒lo=4×20=80 cm.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A set of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn></math> tuning forks is arranged in a series of increasing frequencies. If each fork gives <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math> beats with respect to the preceding fork and the frequency of the last fork is twice the frequency of the first, then the frequency of last fork is _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Hz</mi></math>.
questions.solution:
Each fork produces 4 beats per second with the previous means each fork has frequency 4 Hz more than the previous.

Using relation, flast=ffirst+N-1x, here, N is the number of tuning fork in series and x is beat frequency between two successive forks.

2f0=f0+ 20-14

⇒2f0=f0+76⇒f0=76 Hz

Frequency of last tuning fork is 152 Hz.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In an experiment to determine the velocity of sound in air at room temperature using a resonance tube, the first resonance is observed when the air column has a length of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> for a tuning fork of frequency <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>400</mn><mo> </mo><mi>Hz</mi></math> is used. The velocity of the sound at room temperature is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>336</mn><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The third resonance is observed when the air column has a length of _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cm</mi></math>
questions.solution:
Wavelength of wave ⇒λ=vf=336400=84 cm

At first resonance

λ4=l+e⇒844=20+e, where, e is end correction.

⇒e=1
So third resonance length

5λ4=l2+e

521=l2+1

l2=104 cm
